title: Ray diagrams and curved mirrors
description: Let's practice problems involving formation of images by spherical mirrors, using ray diagrams.

assessment_items.question_content:
A pin is placed $40\ \text{cm}$ in front of a concave mirror of focal length $15\ \text{cm}$.

**Choose the correct characteristics of the image formed by the mirror.**   
*Let's try to figure this out by drawing a ray diagram.*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
##Strategy

The mirror has a focal length $15\ \text{cm}$, so the centre of curvature $\text C$ is $30\ \text{cm}$ from the pole. So, the pin is placed beyond $\text C$. 

We can choose the following rays coming from the top of the pin to draw the ray diagram:

[[☃ image 2]]

- one incident ray parallel to the principal axis, 
- one incident ray passing through $\text F$.

##Solution

[[☃ image 1]]

- The ray parallel to the principal axis passes through $\text F$ after reflection.
- The ray incident through $\text F$ is parallel to the principal axis after reflection.

The reflected rays meet below the principal axis between $\text C$ and $\text F$.

The correct answer is:

The image forms between $\text C$ and $\text F$.

choices.content: The image forms between $\text F$ and $\text P$.
The image is virtual and erect.
The image is magnified in size.
The image forms between $\text C$ and $\text F$.

assessment_items.question_content:
An object is placed at the centre of curvature $\text C$ of a concave mirror.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Choose the correct characteristics of the image formed by the mirror.**   
*Let's try to figure this out by drawing a ray diagram.*

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
##Strategy

Let's choose the following two rays coming from the tip of the object to draw the ray diagram:

[[☃ image 2]]

- one incident ray parallel to the principal axis, 
- one incident ray passing through $\text F$.

**After reflection...**

- the ray parallel to the principal axis passes through $\text F$,
- the ray incident through $\text F$ is parallel to the principal axis.

[[☃ image 1]]

Drawing a perpendicular from the meeting point of the reflected rays to the principal axis gives us the image, which is real, inverted, and of the same size as the object.

The correct answer is:

The image is the same size as the object.

choices.content: The image forms between $\text C$ and $\text F$.
The image is virtual and erect.
The image is the same size as the object.
The image forms at $\text F$.

assessment_items.question_content:
A pencil is placed between the centre of curvature $\text C$ and focus $\text F$ of a large concave mirror.

**Choose the correct characteristics of the image formed by the mirror.**   
*Let's try to figure this out by drawing a ray diagram.*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can choose the following rays coming from the tip of the pencil to draw the ray diagram:

[[☃ image 2]]

- one incident ray parallel to the principal axis, 
- one incident ray passing through $\text F$.

- The ray parallel to the principal axis passes through $\text F$ after reflection.
- The ray incident through $\text F$ is parallel to the principal axis after reflection.

[[☃ image 1]]

We see that a real, inverted and magnified image forms beyond $\text C$.

The correct answer is:

The image is real, inverted, and magnified.

choices.content: The image forms between $\text F$ and $\text P$.
The image is real, inverted, and magnified.
The image is virtual, erect, and magnified.
The image forms between $\text C$ and $\text F$.

assessment_items.question_content:
A candle is placed $10\ \text{cm}$ away from the pole of a concave mirror of focal length $15\ \text{cm}$.

**Choose the correct characteristics of the image formed by the mirror.**   
*Let's try to figure this out by drawing a ray diagram.*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The object distance of the candle ($10\ \text{cm}$) is *smaller* than the focal length of the mirror ($15\ \text{cm}$). So, the candle is placed between $\text F$ and  $\text P$.

We can choose the following rays coming from the tip of the candle to draw the ray diagram:

[[☃ image 2]]

- one incident ray parallel to the principal axis, 
- one incident ray normal to the mirror.

- The ray parallel to the principal axis passes through $\text F$ after reflection.
- The ray incident normally on the mirror passes through $\text C$ after reflection.

[[☃ image 1]]

We find that the reflected rays appear to meet behind the mirror, at a point *higher* than the tip of the candle. Thus we get a virtual and erect image, that is *magnified* in size.

The correct answer is:

The image is magnified in size.

choices.content: The image forms between $\text C$ and $\text F$.
The image is magnified in size.
The image is real.
The image is inverted.

assessment_items.question_content:
An object is placed between the focus $\text F$ and pole $\text P$ of a concave mirror.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Choose the correct characteristics of the image formed by the mirror.**   
*Let's try to figure this out by drawing a ray diagram.*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can choose the following rays coming from the tip of the object to draw the ray diagram:

[[☃ image 2]]

- one incident ray parallel to the principal axis, 
- one incident ray normal to the mirror.

- The ray parallel to the principal axis passes through $\text F$ after reflection.
- The ray incident normally on the mirror passes through $\text C$ after reflection.

[[☃ image 1]]

We find that the reflected rays will never meet in front of the mirror in this case. But they appear to meet *behind the mirror*, meaning the image is virtual. Its also erect and magnified in size.

The correct answer is:

The image is virtual.

choices.content: The image forms beyond $\text C$.
The image is virtual.
The image is inverted.
The image is diminished in size.

assessment_items.question_content:
A pin is placed between the focus $\text F$ and pole $\text P$ of a concave mirror.

**Choose the correct characteristics of the image formed by the mirror.**   
*Let's try to figure this out by drawing a ray diagram.*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can choose the following rays coming from the top of the pin to draw the ray diagram:

[[☃ image 2]]

- one incident ray parallel to the principal axis, 
- one incident ray normal to the mirror.

- The ray parallel to the principal axis passes through $\text F$ after reflection.
- The ray incident normally on the mirror passes through $\text C$ after reflection.

[[☃ image 1]]

We see that the reflected rays appear to meet behind the mirror, and on the same side of the principal axis as the object. So, we get an *erect* image, which is also virtual and magnified.

The correct answer is:

The image is erect.

choices.content: The image forms between $\text F$ and $\text P$.
The image is erect.
The image is real.
The image is diminished in size.

assessment_items.question_content:
A pen is placed on the principal axis of a convex mirror. 

**Choose the correct characteristics of the image formed by the mirror.**   
*Let's try to figure this out by drawing a ray diagram.*

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can choose the following rays coming from the top of the pen to draw the ray diagram:

[[☃ image 1]]

- one incident ray parallel to the principal axis, 
- one incident ray normal to the mirror.

- The ray parallel to the principal axis appears to come from $\text F$ after reflection.
- The ray incident normally on the mirror appears to come from $\text C$ after reflection.

[[☃ image 1]]

We see that the reflected rays will never meet in front of the mirror. But they appear to meet *behind the mirror*, meaning the image is virtual. Its also erect and diminished in size.

The correct answer is:

The image is virtual.

choices.content: The image is virtual.
The image is inverted.
The image is magnified in size.

assessment_items.question_content:
An object is placed in front of a convex mirror, on its principal axis.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Choose the correct characteristics of the image formed by the mirror.**   
*Let's try to figure this out by drawing a ray diagram.*

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
We can choose the following rays coming from the tip of the object to draw the ray diagram:

[[☃ image 1]]

- one incident ray parallel to the principal axis, 
- one incident ray normal to the mirror.

- The ray parallel to the principal axis appears to come from $\text F$ after reflection.
- The ray incident normally on the mirror appears to come from $\text C$ after reflection.

[[☃ image 1]]

We see that the reflected rays appear to meet behind the mirror at a point lower than the tip of the object. So the image is *diminished* in size, virtual, and erect.

The correct answer is:

The image is diminished in size.

choices.content: The image is diminished in size.
The image is formed in front of the mirror.
The image is inverted.

assessment_items.question_content:
An object is placed in front of a convex mirror, on its principal axis.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Choose the correct characteristics of the image formed by the mirror.**   
*Let's try to figure this out by drawing a ray diagram.*

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
We can choose the following rays coming from the tip of the object to draw the ray diagram:

[[☃ image 1]]

- one incident ray parallel to the principal axis, 
- one incident ray normal to the mirror.

- The ray parallel to the principal axis appears to come from $\text F$ after reflection.
- The ray incident normally on the mirror appears to come from $\text C$ after reflection.

[[☃ image 1]]

We see that the reflected rays appear to meet behind the mirror at a point lower than the tip of the object. So the image is *diminished* in size, virtual, and erect.

The correct answer is:

The image is diminished in size.

choices.content: The image is diminished in size.
The image is formed in front of the mirror.
The image is inverted.
